creator: ashish@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Ashish SHUKLA from comment #10)

I think I figured out the issue you're experiencing. If your sudo package is compiled with NLS option (as pointed by jrm), and you're using a non-English locale (LC_ALL variable), then if the password prompt for your locale is not recognized by the regular expression `eshell-password-prompt-regexp', then you fail to get Emacs' regular password prompt, and instead your password characters will get echo'ed in the *eshell* buffer.

Couple of options:

 - Override eshell-password-prompt-regexp for your locale. If you're not sure how to go about this, please post the output of following commands, and we'll try to provide a workaround:

    $ echo $LC_ALL
    $ echo $LANG
    $ sudo true

   So, if you're taking this route, then the change needs to be submitted to upstream too, so that it gets included in next Emacs release, and help other users.

 - Install security/sudo port with NLS=off, so that sudo prompt gets printed in English which is already recognized by GNU Emacs.

 - Switch to an English (or some other locale) which is implemented by `eshell-password-prompt-regexp', or just run Emacs under the locale, e.g. env LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 emacs

Thanks to jrm for helping figure this out.

HTH
